sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.
sections.passage: Last winter I tried a local badminton class and found it immediately (1) ...... . I looked online for an (2) ...... for beginner players nearby. (3) ...... , there wasn't one in my town, so I started asking friends for advice. With very little (4) ...... at first, I borrowed some basic (5) ...... and began practising in a school gym. It soon became an (6) ...... way to stay active after work. A few months later, we organised a friendly (7) ...... for new members. Now we even have a part-time (8) ...... and our regular (9) ...... come from several neighbourhood clubs. Last month, one of our players won a silver (10) ...... in a regional tournament.

items.sentence: She finds it impossible to put **H** up with people who cheat at games.
items.answer: H – up with people who cheat at games.

items.sentence: The defending champion was knocked **C** out in the second round of the competition.
items.answer: C – out in the second round of the competition.

items.sentence: He used to enjoy cycling, but he has gone **F** off team sports lately.
The coach told the players to carry **E** on with the activity while he answered the phone.
items.answer: E – on with the activity while he answered the phone.

items.sentence: The committee decided to bring **G** the race forward by a week.
items.answer: G – the race forward by a week.

items.sentence: Have you ever considered taking **A** up a martial art, to defend yourself.
items.answer: A – up a martial art, to defend yourself.

items.sentence: Even at a young age, Marcus took **B** to swimming as soon as he tried it.
items.answer: B – to swimming as soon as she tried it.

items.sentence: The cyclist had to pull **D** out of the race because of a last minute injury.
items.answer: D – out of the race because of a last minute injury.

sections.title: C: Complete each second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
The tennis match kicked off at noon and the players were still on court at three. **been**
At three o'clock, the players ...... three hours.

Jake never used to spend so many evenings playing video games. **use**
Jake ...... spend so many evenings playing video games.
